title: delphinium parryi subsp. purpureum
intro: Delphinium parryi subsp. purpureum, also known as Parry's larkspur, is a perennial plant species in the delphinium genus, characterized by its tall spikes of showy purple flowers and deeply lobed leaves. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Delphinium parryi subsp. purpureum include its ability to treat respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as its potential to reduce inflammation and alleviate pain. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made into teas, tinctures, and infusions, which are often used to treat various ailments, although it's essential to consult with a healthcare professional before using it for medicinal purposes.
